points.text: Acumen Pharmaceuticals presented data on bispecific antibodies targeting Aβ oligomers and transferrin receptors demonstrating enhanced brain delivery in cynomolgus monkeys at AAIC 2026.

Genmab secured European Commission approval for TEPKINLY (epcoritamab) plus lenalidomide and rituximab as the first bispecific-based therapy for relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma in the second-line setting.
Molecular Partners initiated dosing in a randomized Phase 2 TACTIC trial evaluating MP0317, a bispecific radio-DARPin, combined with chemoimmunotherapy for first-line cholangiocarcinoma.
Genmab's TEPKINLY + R2 regimen demonstrated a 79% reduction in risk of disease progression or death (HR 0.21) versus R2 alone in the Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 trial for follicular lymphoma.
Molecular Partners presented data on bispecific radio-DARPins at the Gordon Research Conference, highlighting the clinical progression of MP0317 in biliary tract carcinoma.

Samsung Biologics — Bispecific antibodies — Platform strategy — Engineering approaches integrating molecular design with process development aim to resolve chain mispairing and heterogeneity while improving yields and stability for scalable manufacturing.

Immatics N.V. — PRAME franchise — Melanoma — Company identified as global leader in precision targeting of PRAME with multiple clinical-stage programs spanning cell therapies and bispecifics; Phase 3 SUPRAME trial for anzu-cel on track for BLA submission in 1H 2027.

Acumen Pharmaceuticals — Bispecific anti-Aβ oligomer/transferrin receptor antibodies — Alzheimer's Disease — Preclinical data presented at AAIC 2026 showing enhanced brain delivery in cynomolgus monkeys via transferrin receptor-mediated transport.

Genmab — TEPKINLY (epcoritamab) + lenalidomide/rituximab — Relapsed/Refractory Follicular Lymphoma — EC approved as first bispecific-based therapy in 2L; Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 showed 79% risk reduction (HR 0.21) and 96% ORR vs 81% for R2 alone.
Molecular Partners — MP0317 — Cholangiocarcinoma — Randomized Phase 2 TACTIC trial initiated with 75-patient target (50 experimental, 25 control); dosing ongoing with data update expected 2027.

Samsung Biologics — Bispecific antibody platforms — Oncology/Complex diseases — Developed engineering strategies to promote correct heavy- and light-chain assembly, reducing downstream purification burden and maintaining biological activity in preclinical models.

Immatics N.V. — Anzu-cel (anzutresgene autoleucel, IMA203) — Metastatic Melanoma — Phase 1b data: 56% confirmed ORR, 14.6 months mDOR, 6.1 months mPFS, 16.2 months mOS in heavily pretreated patients; Phase 3 SUPRAME trial advancing toward BLA submission in 1H 2027.

Acumen Pharmaceuticals — Enhanced Brain Delivery (EBD) technology commercialization — Bispecific anti-Aβ oligomer/transferrin receptor antibodies — Collaboration with JCR Pharmaceuticals ongoing; preclinical data presented July 2026.

Genmab — Bispecific therapy commercialization in hematology — TEPKINLY (epcoritamab) — Secured EC approval for 2L follicular lymphoma indication based on Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 data showing superior PFS and ORR.

Molecular Partners — Bispecific radio-DARPin development — MP0317 — Advanced to Phase 2 TACTIC trial in cholangiocarcinoma; showcasing platform at Gordon Research Conference with trial completion projected for 2028.

Samsung Biologics — Bispecific antibody manufacturability — Platform technology — Director-level team (Wooseok Yang, Kihong Kim) advancing molecular engineering to optimize developability and biological performance for next-generation targeted therapies.

Immatics N.V. — Precision targeting of PRAME via cell therapies and bispecifics — Anzu-cel (cell therapy) + Bispecific pipeline — Phase 3 SUPRAME trial for anzu-cel on track for BLA submission in 1H 2027; clinical-stage programs spanning both modalities.

Bispecific anti-Aβ oligomer/transferrin receptor antibodies — Mechanism: Dual binding to Aβ oligomers and transferrin receptors for BBB transport — Phase: Preclinical — Population: Cynomolgus monkeys — Endpoints/Results: Enhanced brain delivery demonstrated via transferrin receptor-mediated transport system.

TEPKINLY (epcoritamab) — Mechanism: Bispecific T-cell engager (CD3xCD19) — Phase: 3 (EPCORE FL-1) — Population: Relapsed/refractory follicular lymphoma — Comparator: Lenalidomide + rituximab (R2) — Results: 74% complete response rate vs 43% for R2; 79% risk reduction in progression or death.

MP0317 — Mechanism: Bispecific radio-DARPin (immune modulating) — Phase: 2 (TACTIC) — Population: Advanced biliary tract carcinoma (cholangiocarcinoma) — Comparator: Durvalumab + gemcitabine-cisplatin vs SoC alone — Endpoint: Clinical benefit assessment; proof-of-mechanism established in Phase 1 via tumor microenvironment remodeling.

Bispecific antibody formats — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement for complex disease modulation — Phase: Preclinical/Platform development — Population: Tumor models — Results: Effective modulation of disease-associated signaling pathways, inhibition of tumor cell proliferation, and sustained antitumor activity without compromising target binding.

Anzu-cel (anzutresgene autoleucel, IMA203) — Mechanism: PRAME TCR T-cell therapy — Phase: 1b (RP2D cohort) — Population: Heavily pretreated metastatic melanoma (cutaneous and uveal) — Comparator: None (single-arm) — Results: 56% confirmed ORR, 14.6 months mDOR, 6.1 months mPFS, 16.2 months mOS; rapid, deep, durable systemic anti-tumor activity.

Genmab — Regulatory milestone — TEPKINLY (epcoritamab) — European Commission granted marketing authorization for bispecific-based combination therapy in follicular lymphoma, establishing first-in-class status in the region.
Molecular Partners — Clinical trial initiation — MP0317 — Launched investigator-initiated Phase 2 TACTIC study in France with 9 active sites; poster presentation accepted for ESMO 2026.
